steps.ttsScript: Here are three steps for when a word does not make sense.
Step one. Stop.
Step two. Think. Does that word fit the rest of the sentence?
Step three. Try again with a word that makes sense.

slides.heading: Stop, Think, Try Again
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child with a thought bubble above their head containing a tiny lightbulb, looking down at a book thoughtfully. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. You read, the cat sat on the mat.
steps.displayText: The cat sat on the mat.

steps.ttsScript: But you said, the cat sat on the map. Uh oh!
Does a cat sit on a map? Stop. Think. That does not make sense.
Try again. The cat sat on the mat. That makes sense!

slides.heading: Spot the Mix-Up
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon orange tabby cat sitting on a soft green rug in a cozy living room. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
After every sentence, ask yourself, did that make sense?
Now you are ready to practice using clues to read!

slides.heading: Always Ask
slides.imagePrompt: A friendly cartoon owl wearing round glasses, tilting its head as if asking a question, with a tiny thought bubble containing a question mark. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some words have more than one meaning!
The word bat can be a baseball bat, or a little flying animal.
Let us learn how to tell which meaning fits.

slides.heading: One Word, Two Meanings
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon baseball bat standing next to a tiny friendly cartoon bat animal with big eyes, both smiling at each other. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Read the whole sentence. It gives you a clue.
The boy swung the bat at the ball.
Which bat? The baseball bat!

slides.heading: The Sentence Helps
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child swinging a baseball bat on a sunny day in a green park, smiling brightly. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. The bat flew out of the cave at night.
Did a baseball bat fly? No!
This bat is the flying animal. The sentence gave us the clue.

slides.heading: Try Another
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon small brown bat animal flying under a friendly full moon, stars twinkling around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When a word could mean two things, read the whole sentence for clues.
Now you are ready to be a word meaning detective!

slides.heading: Be a Word Detective
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon detective child with a magnifying glass looking at an open book, tiny question mark above their head. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The sentence itself often hides clues about hard words.
Those clues help you guess what the word means.
Let us try it!

slides.heading: Clues Inside a Sentence
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon detective child with a magnifying glass hovering over a sentence on an open page, tiny stars sparkling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. The fluffy cat curled up in the cozy blanket.
What does fluffy mean? Soft and puffy.
How did we know? The blanket and the cat. Those were clues!

slides.heading: The Clue in the Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child looking at a big fluffy orange cat curled up in a cozy blanket, warm and happy. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. The kid bounced high on the trampoline.
What does bounced mean? Jumped up and down!
The trampoline was the clue!

slides.heading: Clue Hunt
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid jumping on a giant trampoline in a backyard, smiling with joy, tiny arrows showing the bouncing. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Read the whole sentence, not just the tricky word!
Now you are ready to use sentence clues!

slides.heading: Read All the Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl with glasses holding a tiny magnifying glass over a page full of friendly sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Little word parts can change what a word means.
They stick on the beginning or the end.
Let us meet a few!

slides.heading: Little Word Parts
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child holding three colorful word-tile pieces like puzzle blocks, smiling as they fit together. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The ending E R can mean one who does.
Teacher? One who teaches!
Runner? One who runs!

slides.heading: The ER Ending
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon teacher with glasses and a clipboard, smiling warmly in a bright classroom, one small chalkboard nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The beginning U N means not.
Unhappy? Not happy!
Unlock? Not locked!

slides.heading: The UN Beginning
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon happy face character on the left with a line between it and a sad face on the right, connected by a tiny arrow. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you see a long word, look at the ends. The little parts give you meaning!
Now you are ready to read word beginnings and endings!

slides.heading: Check the Ends
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass character examining a colorful word, zooming in on the ends. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A root word is the main part of a word.
Other parts can stick on to it.
But the root stays the same.

slides.heading: The Root Word
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon tree with colorful roots visible below the ground, each root glowing softly with little friendly letters around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Look at these words.
What is the same in all three?
Jump! That is the root word.
I N G, E D, and S are the extra parts.

slides.heading: Jumping and Jumped
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon bunny with big floppy ears jumping over a tiny tree stump, with motion arrows showing the jump. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Playing. Played. Plays.
What is the root word? Play!
The endings change, but play stays!

slides.heading: Play Play Play
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child bouncing a bright red ball in the sunshine, smiling widely, soft grass below. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Cover up the ending. What is left? That is the root!
Now you are ready to find root words!

slides.heading: Find the Root
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass character zooming in on a shiny root word at the center of a word tree. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Words are not all alone. They connect to other words.
Some words are opposites. Others mean almost the same thing.
Let us explore!

slides.heading: Words Have Friends
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon word-tile characters holding hands, one with a big smile and another with a curious face, a tiny heart floating between them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Opposites are words that mean very different things.
Hot and cold. Big and small. Fast and slow.
What is the opposite of hot? Cold!

slides.heading: Opposites
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon hot sun character on one side and a friendly cold snowflake character on the other, connected by a tiny line with a lightning bolt between them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some words mean almost the same thing.
Happy. Glad. Cheerful. All feel about the same!
We call those same family words!

slides.heading: Same Family Words
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon happy faces of different styles standing in a row, all smiling brightly, tiny hearts floating nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask, is this word the opposite, or is it close in meaning?
Now you are ready to connect words!

slides.heading: Match and Mix
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child holding two word-tile cards and smiling, with one tile glowing. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Many words belong to the same group.
A group is a name for things that go together.
Let us sort some words!

slides.heading: Word Groups
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child organizing colorful toy animals into three friendly wooden bins, smiling proudly. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. Cat. Dog. Fish. Bird.
What do they have in common?
They are all animals! That is the group.

slides.heading: Animals
slides.imagePrompt: Four cheerful cartoon animals in a friendly row, a cat, a dog, a goldfish, and a little songbird, each with big friendly eyes. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Apple. Banana. Orange. Grape.
What group? Fruits!

slides.heading: Fruits
slides.imagePrompt: Four cheerful cartoon fruits in a row, a red apple, a banana, an orange, and a bunch of grapes, each with tiny smiling faces. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask, what do these words all have in common? That is their group!
Now you are ready to sort words into groups!

slides.heading: Find What Is the Same
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl with glasses pointing at three matching colorful shapes. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A good definition tells two things.
What group is it in? And what is special about it?
Let us try defining a dog.

slides.heading: What Is a Dog?
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon golden retriever dog with big friendly eyes, smiling and panting, sitting on green grass. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A dog is an animal. That is its group.
But not every animal is a dog.
A dog is an animal that barks and wags its tail. That is special about dogs!

slides.heading: Group + Detail
slides.imagePrompt: A friendly cartoon dog next to a small thought bubble showing other animals, and another bubble showing the dog barking happily. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: An apple is a fruit. That is the group.
An apple is a fruit that is round and grows on a tree.
Now we have a clear definition!

slides.heading: Define an Apple
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon shiny red apple with a green leaf, on a small wooden table next to a tiny plate. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Give the group name, and one special thing. That is a great definition!
Now you are ready to define words like a pro!

slides.heading: Two-Part Definition
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon puzzle character with two pieces snapping together, a tiny group icon on one and a special-feature star on the other. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Words are not just in books. They live all around us!
Good readers match words with real-life things they know.
Let us make some connections!

slides.heading: Words Live in Real Life
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child curled up on a cozy armchair with a blanket and a warm drink, smiling peacefully. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The word cozy means warm and comfortable.
What is cozy in real life?
A soft blanket. A warm fireplace. A snuggly bed.

slides.heading: Cozy
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kitten curled up on a warm fluffy blanket by a softly glowing fireplace, eyes closed with a tiny smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Chilly means a little bit cold.
What feels chilly?
Early morning air. A cool breeze. An ice cube!

slides.heading: Chilly
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child wearing a scarf and puffy coat, rubbing their hands together in the snow, breath making a small cloud. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you meet a new word, picture a real thing that matches it!
Now you are ready to connect words to real life!

slides.heading: Picture It
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child with a thought bubble containing three tiny pictures of everyday life. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some words mean almost the same thing. But one is stronger!
Like paint colors. Some are soft. Some are super bright.
Words have shades too!

slides.heading: Shades of Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon paint color card with three shades of blue side by side, from light to very deep, each with a little happy face. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Big means something is large.
Huge means even bigger than big!
An elephant is big. A whale is huge!

slides.heading: Big and Huge
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon elephant standing next to a tiny cartoon whale, the whale labeled in a way that makes it feel much bigger, with tiny sparkles around the whale. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Happy means you feel good.
Thrilled means super, super happy!
A little smile, or a big jumping cheer. Different shades!

slides.heading: Happy and Thrilled
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child smiling softly on the left and the same child jumping in the air with arms wide and a huge grin on the right. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Strong words paint a stronger picture. Pick the shade that fits!
Now you are ready to pick between strong and mild words!

slides.heading: Pick the Right Shade
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child with a paint palette full of colorful paint blobs, considering which color to pick. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Great readers and writers pick the just-right word.
The right word makes a sentence clear!
Let us practice.

slides.heading: Pick the Right Word
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child choosing between two colorful word bubbles floating above their head, both smiling faces on them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: When you read a sentence with a blank, think about what fits.
The kids were hungry. They ate a ___.
A snack? Yes! A song? No!

slides.heading: Match the Meaning
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child placing a word card into a matching empty slot on a sentence card, smiling proudly. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every day, you learn new words from books and talks.
Try using them! The more you use a word, the better you know it.
Big words make your ideas shine!

slides.heading: Use New Words You Know
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl wearing glasses and a small scholar hat, holding a fancy glowing word with sparkles around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask, does this word fit the meaning of my sentence?
Now you are ready to use big words!

slides.heading: Think, Then Pick
slides.imagePrompt: A friendly cartoon brain character with a graduation cap, holding a magnifying glass up to a word choice. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Great readers dig for clues in every story.
We ask smart questions and hunt for the answers.
Let us learn what to ask!

slides.heading: Dig Into Stories
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child wearing a detective hat and magnifying glass, studying an open storybook. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Detectives ask six big questions.
Who. What. Where. When.
And the hardest two. Why. And how!

slides.heading: The Big Six
slides.imagePrompt: Six cheerful cartoon question mark characters in a friendly row, each a different bright color. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. Max the dog saw a red ball in the yard. He ran to fetch it.
Who? Max the dog!
What did he do? He ran to fetch the ball.
Where? In the yard! Answers right in the words.

slides.heading: Try It On a Story
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid reading a book under a tree, with tiny thought bubbles showing a dog and a ball. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
If you are not sure, look back at the words. The answer is usually hiding there.
Now you are ready to ask story detective questions!

slides.heading: Find It in the Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass hovering over an open book with tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some stories are old tales passed down for ages.
Fables and folktales always teach us something important.
That something is called the lesson or moral.

slides.heading: Tales Teach Us
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon storytelling grandparent with warm brown skin reading to two smiling grandkids on a rug. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Remember the tortoise and the hare?
The rabbit was fast, but he stopped to nap.
The slow tortoise kept going and won!
The lesson? Slow and steady wins the race!

slides.heading: The Tortoise and the Hare
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon green tortoise crossing a finish line ahead of a surprised brown rabbit, both smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Retelling is telling a tale in your own words.
Start with the characters. Then what happened. Then the lesson!
Keep the big parts. Skip tiny details.

slides.heading: Retell It
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child holding a book open, telling the story to younger siblings, animated hands. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask, what did the character learn? That is your lesson!
Now you are ready to find story lessons!

slides.heading: Find the Lesson
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl wearing a small graduation cap sitting on a book. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: When things happen in a story, characters react.
They might feel happy, scared, angry, or proud.
How a character reacts tells us who they are.

slides.heading: Characters Have Feelings
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon group of kids of different skin tones showing different facial expressions, from happy to worried to excited. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sam broke his favorite toy. He was heartbroken.
Heartbroken shows how deep his feeling was.
Then his friend fixed it. He was overjoyed!

slides.heading: Listen to Their Reaction
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child looking worried at a broken toy, then smiling brightly as a friend helps. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Big problems show how characters handle tough moments.
Maya had a huge pile of homework. She did not give up.
She is the kind of character who stays brave!

slides.heading: When Problems Come
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child facing a tall stack of books, rolling up their sleeves determined. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When something happens, watch what the character does next. That is their reaction!
Now you are ready to describe how characters react!

slides.heading: Watch the Reactions
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass over a tiny thought cloud containing a smiling face. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Writers pick words that sing. Words with a beat and sounds.
Some words rhyme. Some words bounce.
Let us listen to a few.

slides.heading: Words Have Music
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child wearing headphones, reading a book with musical notes floating out of the pages. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Rhymes are words that sound the same at the end.
Cat and hat. Boom and zoom. Rhymes!
A beat is when lines have the same number of claps.

slides.heading: Rhymes and Beats
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon words shaped like musical notes, connected by a tiny heart. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some words pull in our senses.
The warm sun kissed her face. We feel warm!
The thunder boomed. We hear it!

slides.heading: Sensory Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon bright glowing sun with rays warming a smiling girl's face. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Read poems out loud. You will hear the music!
Now you are ready to hear words that sing!

slides.heading: Read It Aloud
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon microphone with a smiling face, sparkles around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every story has a shape. A beginning, middle, and end.
The shape helps us follow along.
Let us look at each part.

slides.heading: Stories Have Shape
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon mountain outline rising up to a peak and coming back down, with a little trail winding along it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The beginning introduces us. Who is the story about? Where are they?
It sets up the whole story.

slides.heading: The Beginning
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon sunrise over a small cottage where a smiling child is waking up. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The middle is where most of the action happens.
There is usually a problem or a big event.
The end wraps things up. How did it all turn out?

slides.heading: The Middle and End
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon path curving through a green valley, with a smiling child crossing a small bridge in the middle. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
As you read, ask: am I in the beginning, middle, or end?
Now you are ready to see the shape of a story!

slides.heading: Map the Shape
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon curved arrow rising, peaking, and coming down, with three tiny stars marking beginning, middle, end. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: In a story, each character has their own way of seeing things.
That is called their point of view.
Two characters can see the same thing very differently!

slides.heading: Every Character Sees Differently
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon kids standing on either side of a tree, looking at the same scene from different angles, both smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Tom loves the park. He thinks it is the best place ever!
Lea feels lonely at the park. She wishes her friend was there.
Same park, two very different points of view!

slides.heading: Two Views of a Park
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon sunny park with a smiling child playing and another kid sitting sadly watching them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A character's point of view comes from what they feel and want.
Listen to what they say. Watch what they do.
Those are clues to their point of view!

slides.heading: Listen for Their Feelings
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child reading a book with two tiny thought bubbles over their head showing different reactions. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Imagine you are the character. How would you feel?
Now you are ready to see different points of view!

slides.heading: Walk in Their Shoes
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon pair of shoes with tiny stars sparkling around them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Pictures are part of the story too.
They show details the words might not say.
Let us use them together!

slides.heading: Pictures Add to Stories
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon open picture book with a bright illustration leaping off the page. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The words might say: it was cold.
The picture shows snow, bare trees, and a deer in a coat.
Now we know it is winter!

slides.heading: See the Setting
slides.imagePrompt: A cozy cartoon winter forest with smiling deer, bare trees, and gently falling snow. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The picture shows how the character feels.
A huge smile means excited or proud.
Crossed arms and a frown? Maybe angry or upset.

slides.heading: See the Feelings
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child sitting on a school bus with a big proud smile on their first day. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you read, pause on each picture. Ask, what does it add?
Now you are ready to use pictures to understand stories!

slides.heading: Pause and Look
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child pausing to look at an illustration with wide curious eyes. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The same story can be told in different ways.
Different authors. Different pictures. Sometimes different endings!
Let us see what stays the same.

slides.heading: Stories Get Retold
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon open books next to each other with the same cartoon character drawn in two styles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Cinderella has been told all over the world.
Some versions have a fairy godmother. Some have a magic fish!
The main story is the same. The details change.

slides.heading: Cinderella Everywhere
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon Cinderella-like figure with warm brown skin in a sparkly dress and glass slipper, smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: What is the same? The main characters. The big problem.
What is different? The setting. The small parts. The ending sometimes.
Both ways can be fun in their own way!

slides.heading: Compare Two
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon Venn diagram with two overlapping circles containing tiny smiling faces, with a tiny star in the overlap. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Make two lists. Same on one, different on the other.
Now you are ready to compare different story versions!

slides.heading: Same + Different
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid with a clipboard and pencil, checking off items on a comparison list. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Second graders are ready for lots of stories.
Short ones. Long ones. Funny and serious.
Mixing them up helps your reading grow.

slides.heading: A Year of Stories
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon calendar with a different colored book on each month, a smiling reader below. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: This year you will meet chapter books.
Chapter books break the story into smaller pieces.
You can read one or two chapters a day!

slides.heading: Chapter Books Are Coming
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon thick chapter book with a bookmark and a small kid leaning against it smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some books feel easy. That is great for practice.
Some books feel a little hard. Those are stretch books.
Stretch books make your reading stronger!

slides.heading: Stretch Books
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child stretching up tall to grab a book from a high shelf, smiling with effort. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Find a reading spot. Read there every day.
Now you are ready to read stories all year!

slides.heading: Read Every Day
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon cozy armchair with a stack of colorful books beside it, a warm lamp glowing. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Smart readers ask questions as they read facts.
Six big ones: who, what, where, when, why, how.
steps.displayText: Who, what, where, when, why, how

steps.ttsScript: Every one has an answer in the text.

slides.heading: Smart Fact Questions
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child holding a clipboard studying a friendly cartoon animal, thinking hard. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Who, what, where, and when are easy to spot.
Why and how need deeper reading.
They help you really understand the facts.

slides.heading: Why and How Matter Most
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon giant question marks side by side, one saying Why and one saying How with tiny happy faces. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. Seal pups are born on ice. Their moms teach them to swim.
Who? The seal pups! Where? On ice!
Who teaches them? Their moms. Why? So they can survive!

slides.heading: Try It
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon baby seal pup splashing in cold blue water with a mama seal smiling nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Jot questions on sticky notes as you read. Then hunt for the answers!
Now you are ready to ask smart fact questions!

slides.heading: Write Questions
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon sticky note pad with a question mark on each page, a smiling pencil. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Longer fact texts have lots of little ideas.
But one big idea holds them all together.
That big idea is called the main topic.

slides.heading: Find the Big Idea
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon giant lightbulb glowing brightly at the center of a desk, with papers around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Each paragraph focuses on a smaller part of the big idea.
Paragraph one might tell what something is.
Paragraph two might tell why it matters.
All paragraphs point back to the main topic.

slides.heading: Each Paragraph Focuses
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon paragraph blocks in a column, each glowing a different color. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Imagine a text about honey bees.
One paragraph on how they make honey. Another on where they live.
Main topic? Honey bees! Everything connects back to them.

slides.heading: Honey Bees
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon honey bee buzzing near a flower with a tiny honey jar. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
After you read, ask: what is this text mostly about?
Now you are ready to find the big idea!

slides.heading: Ask What It Is All About
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl tilting its head asking a question, with a smile and glasses. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books often connect things to each other.
Two events. Two steps. Two ideas.
Let us learn how to spot the connections!

slides.heading: Connections Everywhere
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon timeline with tiny historical and scientific icons connected by glowing lines. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sequence means the order things happen.
First this. Then that. Last another thing.
Look for words like first, next, and finally.

slides.heading: First, Next, Last
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon numbered arrows in a row, each glowing a different bright color. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: First, a butterfly lays an egg.
Next, a caterpillar hatches.
Then it forms a cocoon.
Finally, a butterfly comes out! Each step connects to the next.

slides.heading: Butterfly Life Cycle
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon cycle diagram with an egg, caterpillar, cocoon, and butterfly, each smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Spot words like first, next, then, last. They mark the order!
Now you are ready to connect history and science!

slides.heading: Look for Order Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass over a word list highlighting first / next / last. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books sometimes use fancy words.
But the text gives us clues to their meaning.
Let us learn to find them!

slides.heading: New Words, No Fear
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid with a magnifying glass looking at a word on a page, eyes wide with curiosity. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sometimes the text just tells you what a word means.
A habitat is the place where an animal lives.
Those definitions are gold!

slides.heading: Definitions Right There
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon sentence with a tiny definition arrow pointing to it, sparkles around. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Other times you have to use the words around it.
The thirsty camel gulped a lot of water.
Gulp must mean drink fast! The thirst gave the clue.

slides.heading: Context Is Your Friend
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid looking at two words linked by a glowing arrow on a page. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Do not skip hard words. Slow down and look for clues.
Now you are ready to figure out word meanings!

slides.heading: Never Skip
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon highlighter character with a smile, ready to highlight a new word. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books have special parts called text features.
They help you find facts fast.
Let us meet them!

slides.heading: Text Features
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon open fact book with a table of contents, heading, caption, and diagram, all glowing. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Headings are big words at the top of a section.
They tell you what that part is about.
Bold words are important vocabulary.

slides.heading: Headings and Bold
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon page with a big bold heading glowing at the top and a word in bold lower down. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A caption tells you about a picture.
An index is at the back. It lists topics with page numbers.
Use the index like a shortcut!

slides.heading: Captions and Index
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon photograph with a tiny caption underneath, and a tiny index page next to it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Before reading, flip through. Look at headings and pictures.
Now you are ready to find info fast!

slides.heading: Scan First
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon eye scanning across a page with a tiny motion trail. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

title: Why Did the Author Write This?

steps.ttsScript: Every author writes for a reason.
We call that the author's purpose.
Usually it is to teach, to entertain, or to convince.

slides.heading: Authors Have a Purpose
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon author in a cozy writing corner with a lightbulb over their head. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books want to teach us.
They share facts, steps, and pictures that help us learn.

slides.heading: To Teach
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon owl in a scholar's cap teaching from an open book. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Stories entertain us. They make us laugh, cry, or wonder.
Some texts try to convince. They give reasons to agree with the author.

slides.heading: To Entertain or Convince
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon theater mask next to a tiny persuasive megaphone. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you finish, ask: why did the author write this?
Now you are ready to spot author purpose!

slides.heading: Ask the Big Why
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon giant question mark with a small smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: In fact books, pictures are not just decoration.
They show what words might not be able to say easily.
Let us see how!

slides.heading: Pictures Do Heavy Lifting
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon diagram of a flower with tiny labeled parts. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A diagram shows parts of something.
Arrows and labels point to each part.
Easier to see than to read!

slides.heading: Diagrams Show Parts
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon diagram of a butterfly with labeled wings and body. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A map shows where things are.
Photos show real things close up.
Together they make facts come alive!

slides.heading: Maps and Photos
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon world map with cute country shapes and a photo of an animal to the side. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you see a picture, ask: how does this match the text?
Now you are ready to use pictures to understand facts!

slides.heading: Match Picture to Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid pointing at a picture while tracing words with their finger. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

title: How Authors Back Up Their Points

steps.ttsScript: When an author makes a point, they also give reasons.
The reasons back up the point.
Spotting them makes you a strong reader!

slides.heading: Point + Proof
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon judge's gavel next to an open book with a tiny highlighted line. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Point: you should drink water every day.
Reason one: it helps your body work.
Reason two: it keeps you from getting tired.
Two reasons support the point!

slides.heading: Drink Water
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon glass of water with a friendly face and a small checkmark next to it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Point: riding bikes is fun and healthy.
Reasons? Exercise. Fresh air. Going places fast!
The reasons make the point stronger.

slides.heading: Try One
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon bicycle with a small smile, next to a kid wearing a helmet. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Watch for words like because, since, and for example. Those signal reasons!
Now you are ready to spot author's reasons!

slides.heading: Look for Because
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass looking at the word because on a page. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Two fact books can be about the same topic.
But each author picks different details.
Reading both gives you a bigger picture.

slides.heading: Two Takes on One Topic
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon open fact books side by side, both about space, with a tiny bridge of arrows between them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Look for what both books agree on.
Those are the main points everyone knows.

slides.heading: Same Points
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon Venn diagram with two overlapping circles and a star in the middle. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Then look for what is special in each one.
One might share more pictures. The other more stories.
Both add to what you know!

slides.heading: Different Details
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon magnifying glass over a unique detail on one book, while a different detail glows on another. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Make a two-column chart. Same on one, different on the other.
Now you are ready to compare two fact books!

slides.heading: Make a Chart
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon clipboard with two columns labeled Same and Different, tiny checkmarks in each. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Reading fact books all year makes you smarter.
Different topics build different knowledge.

slides.heading: A Year of Facts
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon stack of colorful fact books with a calendar behind them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Pick fact books about things you love.
Animals, sports, space, food, weather. Anything!
Love makes it fun.

slides.heading: Pick What You Love
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid browsing a bookshelf with three tiny thought bubbles of animals, space, and sports. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sometimes try a harder book than usual.
Stretch books grow your reading muscles.

slides.heading: Try Stretch Books
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid on tiptoes reaching for a book on a high shelf. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Try a new topic each week. You will discover something cool!
Now you are ready to read facts all year!

slides.heading: New Topic Each Week
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon calendar with a different colored themed bookmark on each week. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Phonics is knowing the sounds letters make.
When you know phonics, you can read new words all by yourself.
Let us grow your phonics power.

slides.heading: Phonics Power
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon superhero kid with a cape made of letters, standing confidently. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Most letters make one sound.
B buzzes. M hums. T taps.

slides.heading: One Letter, One Sound
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon letter B with a tiny buzz next to it, smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some letters team up to make one sound.
SH in ship. CH in chip. TH in thumb.

slides.heading: Some Letters Team Up
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon letter pairs holding hands, each with a small speech bubble. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you see a new word, sound out each letter or letter team.
Now you are ready to use phonics power!

slides.heading: Trust Your Phonics
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid with a lightbulb above their head, holding a book confidently. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every vowel has two voices: short and long.
Short sounds are quick. Long sounds say the letter's name.
Let us listen.

slides.heading: Two Vowel Voices
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon vowel characters, one short and chunky, one tall and elegant, smiling together. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The word cat has a short A.
Short A says ah. Quick!

slides.heading: Short in CAT
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon smiling orange cat with big eyes. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The word cake has a long A.
Long A says AY. It says its name!
A silent E at the end often signals a long vowel.

slides.heading: Long in CAKE
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon pink frosted cake with a single lit candle. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask: does the vowel say its name? Long. Or does it make a quick sound? Short.
Now you are ready to tell long from short vowels!

slides.heading: Listen Closely
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon ear listening carefully with sound waves. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sometimes two vowels work together.
They make just one sound between them.
Let us meet some vowel teams.

slides.heading: Vowel Teams
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon vowel characters holding hands with a small star between them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: EA says EE, like in eat or read.
EE says EE too, like in bee or tree.
Two different spellings, same sound!

slides.heading: EA and EE
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon pea pod and a bumblebee side by side, both smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: AI says AY, like in rain or train.
OA says OH, like in boat or road.

slides.heading: AI and OA
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon rain cloud and a rowboat on a lake, both smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When two vowels walk together, the first one does the talking!
Now you are ready to read vowel teams!

slides.heading: First Vowel Talks
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon first vowel character speaking into a microphone while the second stands silent behind. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Two-syllable words often have long vowel sounds.
Breaking the word into pieces helps us read them.

slides.heading: Long Words, Long Sounds
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon word tile character stretching tall, with musical notes floating around. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Baseball becomes base and ball.
Base has a long A. The silent E makes it long.
Ball has a short sound. Both pieces together? Baseball!

slides.heading: Break BASEBALL
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon baseball with stitches and a smile on a green field. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Rainbow becomes rain and bow.
Rain has the AI team. Long A sound!
Bow has OW. Another long sound!

slides.heading: Break RAINBOW
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon rainbow arching over a green hill with a tiny sun. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Split. Read each piece. Then blend them back together!
Now you are ready to read two-syllable long vowel words!

slides.heading: Split, Read, Blend
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon pair of scissors splitting a long word tile into two pieces. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Little pieces stick on the front and back of words.
The front ones are prefixes. The back ones are suffixes.
Each one changes the word's meaning!

slides.heading: Word Add-Ons
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon word with tiny prefix and suffix puzzle pieces snapping onto it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: UN means not. Unhappy means not happy.
RE means again. Redo means do again.

slides.heading: UN and RE Prefixes
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon padlock being unlocked with a magical glow. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: ER means one who does. Teacher means one who teaches.
LY turns a word into how. Quickly means in a quick way.

slides.heading: ER and LY Suffixes
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon teacher with a clipboard next to a tiny running character. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you meet a long word, peel off the prefix and suffix. Find the root!
Now you are ready to read prefixes and suffixes!

slides.heading: Peel It Apart
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon banana peeling apart into three pieces with a tiny smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Some spellings are tricky. Letters hide or change sound!
These are surprises English throws at us.
Let us learn a few common ones.

slides.heading: Silent Letters and Surprises
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon letter K with a finger on its lips in a shh pose, a tiny sparkle nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: In knee, the K is silent.
In lamb, the B is silent.
In write, the W is silent too!

slides.heading: Silent Letters
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon knee wearing a pair of glasses, a tiny letter K hiding in a bush nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: When P and H team up, they make the F sound!
Phone. Photo. Dolphin. All PH words that sound like F.

slides.heading: PH Makes F
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon phone character with a friendly smile and tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When a word surprises you, write it down. Then practice it a few times!
Now you are ready to read tricky spellings!

slides.heading: Memorize the Surprises
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon brain with a graduation cap, smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: You already know some sight words. It is time for more!
Second grade sight words show up in every book.

slides.heading: More Sight Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon shelf of colorful sight word flashcards with tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Because is a big word that shows up a lot.
People has a silent O. P-e-o-p-l-e!

slides.heading: Because and People
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon word tiles with happy faces in a row. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Again has an AI but sounds like uh-gen.
Could has a silent L! C-o-u-l-d, sounds like kood.

slides.heading: Again and Could
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon word tiles side by side, each with tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
The more often you see a sight word, the faster you know it.
Now you are ready for more sight words!

slides.heading: See Them Often
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon flashcard character with a friendly smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Great readers read smoothly, like talking.
Fluency is reading accurately, at the right speed, with expression.

slides.heading: Smooth Reading
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child reading out loud on a small stage with warm spotlight. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Accuracy means reading the words correctly.
If you get stuck, slow down and sound it out.

slides.heading: Accuracy First
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon target with an arrow in the bullseye. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Rate is how fast. Not too fast, not too slow.
Expression means using your voice to match the story.

slides.heading: Rate and Expression
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon metronome swinging back and forth with a smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.

After each sentence, ask: did that make sense?

slides.heading: Ask If It Makes Sense

steps.ttsScript: Good readers figure out tricky words.

steps.ttsScript: The sentence around a word gives hints.

steps.ttsScript: The grumpy kid frowned and crossed his arms.
Grumpy must mean unhappy. The frown and crossed arms told us.

Never skip words. The clues are in the whole sentence.

steps.ttsScript: A prefix is a small word piece at the front.

steps.ttsScript: UN means not. Unhappy is not happy.
Unlock, unsafe, unkind — all start with UN.

steps.ttsScript: RE means again. Redo means do again.
Rewrite, reread, replay.

slides.heading: RE Means Again

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you see a long word, peel off the prefix. See if the root word looks familiar!
Now you are ready to use prefix power!

slides.heading: Peel Off the Prefix

steps.ttsScript: The root is the main part of a word.

All share the same meaning of play!

slides.heading: One Root, Many Words

steps.ttsScript: Help. Helper. Helpful. Helpless. All from help!
The root tells you they all connect to helping.

slides.heading: Help and Helpful

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Cover the prefix and suffix. What is left? That is the root!
Now you are ready to use root words!

slides.heading: Cover and Discover

You can use both pieces to guess the meaning!

steps.ttsScript: Rainbow! Rain plus bow.
A bow of color in the rain. Makes sense!

slides.heading: Rainbow

steps.ttsScript: Sunflower. A flower that loves the sun.
Toothbrush. A brush for your teeth.

slides.heading: Sunflower, Toothbrush

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
See a long word? Look for two smaller words inside!

You will find the meaning right there.

slides.heading: Look and Learn

Put a bookmark at the glossary so you can flip there fast!

slides.heading: Use It Often

steps.ttsScript: Words connect to each other in all kinds of ways.

Dog, cat, fish. All animals!

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you meet a new word, think: what words is this similar to?

slides.heading: Make Connections

steps.ttsScript: Words live in real life, not just books.
Great readers match words to things they know.

When you learn a new word, think of a real moment that matches it.

steps.ttsScript: Happy. A small, warm feeling.

Thrilled. You are bursting with it!

Ask: how strong is the feeling? Then pick the word that matches!

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Pick one new word each day. Use it in a sentence!

steps.ttsScript: Sometimes two letters team up to make just one sound!

They make a brand new sound together!

Chip. Chop. Cheese. Hear the "chh"?
And T and H make the "thh" sound. Like in thumb.
Thumb. Thin. Think. Feel your tongue?

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you see S-H, C-H, or T-H together, they make one sound, not two.

slides.heading: Listen for Team Sounds

steps.ttsScript: Short words are made of just a few sounds.

slides.heading: Sound It Out

steps.ttsScript: Let us read this word.
First sound.
Middle sound.
Last sound.
Now blend. Cat!

slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon orange cat sitting upright, smiling, tail curled. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Your turn.
First.
Middle.
Last.
Blend! Dog!

slides.heading: Your Turn: DOG

Say each sound slowly. Then say them faster and faster until they snap together!

steps.ttsScript: Here is a magical trick in reading. It is called Magic E.
Add an E to the end of a word, and something magical happens.
The vowel starts to say its name.

slides.heading: Magic E

steps.ttsScript: The word cap has a short A sound.

slides.heading: Cap Becomes Cape

steps.ttsScript: The word kit has a short I sound.
Add a Magic E. Kit becomes kite!
Now the I says its name: EYE!

slides.heading: Kit Becomes Kite

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
The Magic E is silent. But it is super powerful. It changes the vowel!
Now you are ready to spot Magic E words!

slides.heading: Silent but Powerful

steps.ttsScript: Every word has beats inside it. We call them syllables.
You can clap a word to find the beats!
Let us try some together.

slides.heading: Clap Out the Beats

steps.ttsScript: Listen. Cat.
One clap! Cat has one beat. One syllable.

slides.heading: Cat Is One Beat
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon cat sitting up with one big clap emoji next to it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Listen. Rab-bit.
Two claps! Rabbit has two beats. Two syllables.
Every syllable needs a vowel. Rabbit has A and I. Two vowels, two syllables.

slides.heading: Rabbit Is Two Beats

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Put your hand under your chin. Each time your chin drops, that is one syllable!

slides.heading: Your Hand Knows

steps.ttsScript: Some words have two beats. Two syllables.

Then read each piece!

slides.heading: Bigger Words, Two Beats

steps.ttsScript: If there are two of the same consonants in the middle, split between them.

slides.heading: Split Between the Letters

steps.ttsScript: Let us read pencil together.
Split. Pen and cil.
Read each piece. Pen. Cil.
Now blend. Pencil!

slides.heading: Read PENCIL

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Split the word. Read each piece. Then blend them back together!
Now you are ready to read two-syllable words!

slides.heading: Two Pieces, One Word
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon puzzle pieces fitting together, with a word tile forming from them, tiny sparkles in the middle. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Little endings can change what a word means.

steps.ttsScript: The S ending means more than one.

steps.ttsScript: The ending E D means it already happened.

slides.heading: The -ed and -ing Endings

Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Look at the last letters. The ending tells you when or how many!

slides.heading: Spot the Ending

steps.ttsScript: Most words you can sound out. But a few are tricky!

steps.ttsScript: Look at this word. Said.
