It breaks the rules! So we just memorize it.

slides.heading: Said
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child with a speech bubble above their head containing a happy face. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Was. Were. Have.
Was sounds like wuz, but it ends in s.
Were has a silent E that helps it end in R.
Have ends in a silent E too. Tricky words stick together.

slides.heading: Was, Were, Have
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon word tiles in a friendly row, each with a different color and a tiny smile. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
The more often you see a tricky word, the faster you know it. Flash cards help!
Now you are ready to read tricky sight words!

slides.heading: See Them Every Day
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon word-flashcard character with a friendly smile, stars sparkling around it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Great readers think before they read.
They ask, why am I reading this book?
That question is called your purpose.

slides.heading: Know Why You Read
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child holding an open book with a tiny target or goal symbol floating above the page, smiling confidently. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: We read for two big reasons.
A storybook. We read it for fun!
A fact book. We read it to learn!

slides.heading: Two Big Reasons
slides.imagePrompt: Two cheerful cartoon open books side by side, one a whimsical storybook with a tiny dragon, one a fact book with a tiny globe. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Imagine you want to learn how butterflies fly.
Which book fits? A fact book about butterflies!
Now imagine you want a bedtime story.
A storybook! Your purpose picks your book.

slides.heading: Picking a Book
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child looking at a bookshelf with many colorful books, pointing at one thoughtfully. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Before you open a book, ask yourself: what do I want from this book?
Now you are ready to read with purpose!

slides.heading: Pause Before You Start
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child pausing with a finger on their chin, a tiny lightbulb blinking above their head. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every word in a sentence has a job.
Some words name things. Some show action. Some describe.
Knowing the jobs helps us talk and write clearly!

slides.heading: Word Jobs
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon group of colorful word-tile characters standing in a friendly row, each with a different smiling face. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A noun names a person, place, or thing.
Dog. Tree. School. Mom. All nouns!
If you can point to it, it is usually a noun.

slides.heading: Nouns Name Things
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon items in a friendly row, a smiling dog, a tree, and a small house. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A verb shows action. What someone is doing.
Run. Jump. Clap. Eat. All verbs!
Every sentence needs a verb to tell what is happening.

slides.heading: Verbs Show Action
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon action scenes in a row, a child running, a child jumping, and a child clapping. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Ask: what names a thing? That is the noun. What shows action? That is the verb!
Now you are ready to spot grammar jobs!

slides.heading: Spot the Jobs
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon detective child with a magnifying glass hovering over a sentence card, tiny sparkles where they look. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: When we write, we follow a few important rules.
The rules help our readers understand us.
Let us learn two big ones.

slides.heading: Writing Rules
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child writing on a sheet of paper with a big yellow pencil, smiling, stars floating above the paper. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every sentence starts with a capital letter.
The dog runs. See the big T?
Names also start with capitals. Maya, not maya.

slides.heading: Rule 1: Start Big
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon capital letter T character wearing a small gold crown, standing at the front of a line of lowercase letters. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Every sentence ends with a mark.
A period for telling. A question mark for asking. An exclamation mark for excited.
Pick the mark that fits your sentence!

slides.heading: Rule 2: End With a Mark
slides.imagePrompt: Three cheerful cartoon punctuation mark characters in a row, a period, a question mark, and an exclamation mark, all waving. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
When you write, check the start and the end of every sentence.
Now you are ready to use capitals and periods!

slides.heading: Check Both Ends
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon sentence card with a capital letter glowing at the start and a period glowing at the end, tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Second graders learn more sentence skills.
Collective nouns, irregular verbs, and tricky pronouns.
Let us meet them!

slides.heading: Grammar Grows Up
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon group of letter characters in a line, each holding hands. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: A collective noun names a group as one thing.
Team. Herd. Flock. Family.
Use a singular verb with them. The team runs!

slides.heading: Collective Nouns
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon flock of birds flying together in a V formation, all smiling. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Most past tense verbs add E D. But some are irregular!
Yesterday I ate. I ran. I flew.
Those do not follow the E D rule. Memorize!

slides.heading: Irregular Verbs
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid pointing at a tiny calendar yesterday box with a bird flying. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Say the sentence out loud. Does it sound right? Your ear often knows!
Now you are ready to use strong sentence skills!

slides.heading: Listen for What Sounds Right
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon ear listening carefully with tiny sparkles. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Two tiny marks do big jobs in sentences.
Apostrophes and commas.
Let us learn both!

slides.heading: Tiny Marks, Big Jobs
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon apostrophe and comma character side by side, both with happy faces. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Add an apostrophe and an S to show owning.
The dog's bone. The girl's book.

slides.heading: Apostrophes Show Owning
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon dog sitting proudly next to its bone, with a tiny apostrophe and S floating. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: An apostrophe can also replace missing letters.
Can not becomes can't. Do not becomes don't.

slides.heading: Apostrophes in Contractions
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon two-word pair squishing together into one word with a tiny apostrophe. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Commas separate items in a list.
I like apples, bananas, and grapes!
Now you are ready to use apostrophes and commas!

slides.heading: Commas Separate Lists
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon trio of fruit with tiny cartoon commas between them. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: The words you pick change how your message sounds.
Some words sound friendly. Some sound formal.

slides.heading: Pick Your Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid choosing from a basket of colorful word cards. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Formal words are for teachers, letters, and essays.
Casual words are for friends and family.
Thank you vs. Thanks. Both are nice. One is more formal.

slides.heading: Formal and Casual
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid in formal clothes next to the same kid in casual clothes. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Stronger words paint a stronger picture.
Instead of big, try enormous. Instead of sad, try heartbroken.

slides.heading: Stronger Words
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid flexing a muscle next to a sparkly word. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Think about who will read your words. Then pick words that fit!
Now you are ready to choose words to sound just right!

slides.heading: Know Your Reader
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon kid holding up different letters for different people. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Reading a little every day is like a superpower.
The more stories and poems you read, the stronger your reading gets.
Let us see how to read all year long.

slides.heading: Read Every Day
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon calendar with a tiny bookmark on each month, and a friendly child reading next to it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Pick stories about different things.
Funny ones. Scary ones. Adventure ones. Poems too!
New stories teach you new words and new ideas.

slides.heading: Mix It Up
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon bookshelf full of different-colored books and a child picking one out, with others ready to choose. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Sometimes a story has tricky words or long sentences.
That is a good thing! Growing readers try hard books.
When you need help, ask a grown-up or friend. That is smart!

slides.heading: When It Gets Tricky
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child with a puzzled look, raising a hand with a tiny lightbulb above them, a grown-up smiling nearby. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Pick a time to read every day. Bedtime, after lunch, or right when you wake up.
Now you are ready to enjoy stories all year long!

slides.heading: A Reading Routine
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon clock with hearts around it, a child in pajamas sitting on a bed reading a book. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books are full of real things from our world.
They can be about anything! Animals, space, weather, food.
Let us learn how to read fact books all year.

slides.heading: Facts Are Fun
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child standing on a colorful rug surrounded by different fact books, each open with a tiny diagram, smiling excitedly. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Pick fact books about things you love.
Love bugs? Read about insects. Love space? Read about planets.
You will have so much more fun when it is your favorite topic.

slides.heading: Pick What You Love
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon child thinking, with three tiny thought bubbles around them showing a bug, a planet, and a sports ball. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Fact books have helpers built in.
Headings. Pictures. Labels. Use them to find facts fast.
They are like a roadmap through the book!

slides.heading: Use the Parts
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon open fact book with a colorful heading, a friendly diagram of a plant with tiny labels, and a smiling caption. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.

steps.ttsScript: Here is a helpful trick.
Try a new topic each week. You will discover something you did not know!
Now you are ready to explore fact books all year!

slides.heading: New Topic Every Week
slides.imagePrompt: A cheerful cartoon calendar with tiny themed stickers on each week, a kid reading next to it. Clean pastel background. Bright 2D cartoon illustration, bold clean outlines, vibrant saturated colors. No text, no words, no letters.
